core_thesis: Freud holds that psychic life is not transparent to consciousness: dreams, symptoms, and slips are compromise formations through which repressed wishes find distorted expression ([Interpretation of Dreams ch. 7](cite://freud/the-interpretation-of-dreams/7); [Beyond the Pleasure Principle V](cite://freud/beyond-the-pleasure-principle/5)). Yet the psyche is not ordered simply by pleasure seeking; repetition can exceed satisfaction and return the subject to traumatic impasse. Analysis works by making these conflictual determinations speak rather than by positing a unified self.
